opts: (A) A technique used to clone genes from one organism to another.
(B) A method used to identify the genotype of an organism.
(C) A tool used to analyze the expression of genes in an organism.
(D) A way to transfer characteristics from one species to another.
questions.explanation: Recombinant DNA technology involves the manipulation of DNA molecules to produce new combinations of genes.
questions.topic: Basic Concepts and Applications of Biotechnology

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a recombinant DNA molecule?
opts: (A) A molecule consisting of DNA from two different species.
(B) A molecule consisting of RNA from two different species.
(C) A molecule consisting of DNA from two different organisms of the same species.
(D) A molecule consisting of DNA from one species and proteins from another.
questions.explanation: Recombinant DNA molecules are formed by combining DNA from two different species or organisms.
questions.topic: Basic Concepts and Applications of Biotechnology

questions.q: What is the purpose of cloning in biotechnology?
opts: (A) To create a new species.
(B) To study the genetic makeup of an organism.
(C) To produce large quantities of a desired gene product.
(D) To introduce new characteristics into an organism.
questions.explanation: Cloning allows for the mass production of a desired gene product, such as insulin or growth hormone.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a genetically modified organism (GMO)?
opts: (A) A bacterium that produces insulin.
(B) A plant that has been genetically modified to be resistant to pests.
(C) A mammal that has been genetically modified to be immune to a disease.
(D) A fungus that has been genetically modified to produce antibiotics.
questions.explanation: Genetically modified organisms (GMOs) are organisms that have been altered using genetic engineering techniques.

questions.q: What is the difference between a transgenic organism and a knockout organism?
opts: (A) A transgenic organism has a specific gene deleted, while a knockout organism has a gene inserted.
(B) A transgenic organism has a gene inserted, while a knockout organism has a gene deleted.
(C) A transgenic organism has a gene amplified, while a knockout organism has a gene deleted.
(D) A transgenic organism has a gene mutated, while a knockout organism has a gene amplified.
questions.explanation: A transgenic organism has a gene inserted, while a knockout organism has a gene deleted.

questions.q: What is the purpose of gene editing in biotechnology?
opts: (A) To introduce new characteristics into an organism.
(B) To study the genetic makeup of an organism.
(C) To produce large quantities of a desired gene product.
(D) To correct genetic mutations in an organism.
questions.explanation: Gene editing techniques, such as CRISPR, allow for the precise modification of an organism's genome.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a gene expression system?
opts: (A) A system used to produce insulin in bacteria.
(B) A system used to produce antibodies in yeast.
(C) A system used to study gene regulation in animals.
(D) A system used to produce vaccines in plants.
questions.explanation: Gene expression systems are used to produce specific proteins or peptides in a controlled manner.

questions.q: What is the difference between a prokaryotic and a eukaryotic expression system?
opts: (A) A prokaryotic expression system uses eukaryotic hosts, while a eukaryotic expression system uses prokaryotic hosts.
(B) A prokaryotic expression system uses prokaryotic hosts, while a eukaryotic expression system uses eukaryotic hosts.
(C) A prokaryotic expression system uses viral vectors, while a eukaryotic expression system uses plasmids.
(D) A prokaryotic expression system uses plasmids, while a eukaryotic expression system uses viral vectors.
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic expression systems use prokaryotic hosts, while eukaryotic expression systems use eukaryotic hosts.

questions.q: What is the purpose of protein engineering in biotechnology?
opts: (A) To produce large quantities of a desired protein.
(B) To modify the structure of a protein to improve its function.
(C) To study the structure of a protein.
(D) To produce vaccines in plants.
questions.explanation: Protein engineering involves modifying the structure of a protein to improve its function or improve its performance in a biological system.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a biopharmaceutical?
opts: (A) A vaccine produced using a viral vector.
(B) A protein produced using a prokaryotic expression system.
(C) An antibody produced using a eukaryotic expression system.
(D) A gene therapy product produced using a viral vector.
questions.explanation: Biopharmaceuticals are products that are derived from biological sources, such as proteins, antibodies, or vaccines.

questions.q: What is the difference between a monoclonal and a polyclonal antibody?
opts: (A) A monoclonal antibody is produced using a single cell line, while a polyclonal antibody is produced using multiple cell lines.
(B) A monoclonal antibody is produced using multiple cell lines, while a polyclonal antibody is produced using a single cell line.
(C) A monoclonal antibody is produced using a eukaryotic expression system, while a polyclonal antibody is produced using a prokaryotic expression system.
(D) A monoclonal antibody is produced using a prokaryotic expression system, while a polyclonal antibody is produced using a eukaryotic expression system.
questions.explanation: Monoclonal antibodies are produced using a single cell line, while polyclonal antibodies are produced using multiple cell lines.

questions.q: What is the purpose of nanotechnology in biotechnology?
opts: (A) To improve the delivery of drugs using nanoparticles.
(B) To study the structure of cells using nanoscale imaging techniques.
(C) To produce large quantities of a desired protein using nanotechnology.
(D) To modify the structure of a protein using nanotechnology.
questions.explanation: Nanotechnology is used to improve the delivery of drugs using nanoparticles, which can target specific cells or tissues.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a biosensor?
opts: (A) A device used to detect the presence of a specific protein in a sample.
(B) A device used to detect the presence of a specific gene in a sample.
(C) A device used to detect the presence of a specific virus in a sample.
(D) A device used to detect the presence of a specific bacterium in a sample.
questions.explanation: Biosensors are devices that can detect the presence of a specific protein or other biomolecule in a sample.

questions.q: What is the difference between a biochip and a biosensor?
opts: (A) A biochip is a device that can detect the presence of a specific protein, while a biosensor is a device that can detect the presence of a specific gene.
(B) A biochip is a device that can detect the presence of a specific gene, while a biosensor is a device that can detect the presence of a specific protein.
(C) A biochip is a device that can detect the presence of a specific protein, while a biosensor is a device that can detect the presence of a specific bacterium.
(D) A biochip is a device that can detect the presence of a specific bacterium, while a biosensor is a device that can detect the presence of a specific protein.
questions.explanation: A biochip is a device that can detect the presence of a specific gene, while a biosensor is a device that can detect the presence of a specific protein.

questions.q: What is the purpose of synthetic biology in biotechnology?
opts: (A) To design and construct new biological systems, such as microorganisms or biological pathways.
(B) To study the structure of cells using nanoscale imaging techniques.
(C) To produce large quantities of a desired protein using synthetic biology.
(D) To modify the structure of a protein using synthetic biology.
questions.explanation: Synthetic biology involves the design and construction of new biological systems, such as microorganisms or biological pathways.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a synthetic biological system?
opts: (A) A genetically modified bacterium that produces a specific protein.
(B) A genetically modified yeast that produces a specific protein.
(C) A genetically modified mammal that produces a specific protein.
(D) A genetically modified plant that produces a specific protein.
questions.explanation: Synthetic biological systems are designed and constructed using genetic engineering techniques.

questions.q: What is the difference between a horizontal gene transfer and a vertical gene transfer?
opts: (A) A horizontal gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another of the same species, while a vertical gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another of a different species.

(B) A horizontal gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another of a different species, while a vertical gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another of the same species.
(C) A horizontal gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another, while a vertical gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one generation to the next.
(D) A horizontal gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one generation to the next, while a vertical gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another.
questions.explanation: Horizontal gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one organism to another, while vertical gene transfer involves the transfer of genes from one generation to the next.

questions.q: What is the purpose of gene silencing in biotechnology?
opts: (A) To introduce new characteristics into an organism.
(B) To study the genetic makeup of an organism.
(C) To produce large quantities of a desired gene product.
(D) To block the expression of a specific gene in an organism.
questions.explanation: Gene silencing involves the blocking of gene expression, either by degrading mRNA or by preventing transcription.
